hypothesis: Repeated XR rejection can be reduced if every `sysopt.xr-*` backlog card is validated against the authoritative `/work/xr` gap artifact and evaluator focus contract before route lanes act on it.
measurement: Before the ratchet, `CLAW/scripts/verify-systems-optimizer.mjs` enforced `/work/xr` gap binding only for active, kept, or rejected cards in state, so a backlog `sysopt.xr-*` item could omit `target_route`, `target_gap`, or `evaluator_focus` and still verify clean. After the ratchet, the verifier applies the same XR gap contract to backlog items and the fixed promotion bundle stays green.
keep_rule: Keep only if malformed backlog `sysopt.xr-*` cards are rejected for missing `/work/xr` gap binding or evaluator focus, while the declared scope-local evaluation bundle passes without touching route implementation paths.
learning_item: XR systems-optimizer ratchets must fail closed at backlog time; if the authoritative gap binding is optional before execution, route lanes can inherit vague XR work orders.
